computedResults: CI and task artifacts; never committed refresh obligations

guardrail-policy.claim: guardrail registry and CI entrypoints agree
guardrail-policy.trigger: guardrail registry, workflow, or harness/ci entrypoint changes
guardrail-policy.failSafe: CI entrypoint changes also expand the fast selector to the full retained inventory
guardrail-policy.escalation: remove fields or merge entries if policy maintenance becomes larger than reconciliation
guardrail-policy.failureClass: CI can silently skip or misclassify a consequential guard
guardrail-policy.representativeRegression: missing gate mappings, invalid blocking metadata, and unknown selector categories fail the validator

rust.claim: Rust formatting, compilation, clippy, and workspace tests
rust.failSafe: unknown or CI changes expand to the full inventory
rust.cost: large; 21.817 seconds in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
rust.escalation: split only when a narrower command preserves affected-workspace coverage
rust.failureClass: authoritative Rust code does not compile, lint, or satisfy its behavior regressions
rust.representativeRegression: an injected Rust gate failure propagates through the changed-surface selector

typescript.claim: TypeScript build, typecheck, tests, lint, and package boundaries
typescript.failSafe: unknown or CI changes expand to the full inventory
typescript.cost: large; 46.334 seconds in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
typescript.escalation: split only when affected-package selection retains package-boundary coverage
typescript.failureClass: consumer-facing TypeScript no longer builds, typechecks, or obeys authority/import boundaries
typescript.representativeRegression: an injected TypeScript gate failure propagates through the changed-surface selector

contracts.failSafe: protocol changes also select Rust, TypeScript, and bridge validation
contracts.cost: small; 1.598 seconds in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
contracts.escalation: narrow by protocol family only after stale generated output still fails reliably
contracts.failureClass: generated consumer contracts disagree with Rust wire source truth
contracts.representativeRegression: fresh code generation must byte-match committed generated contracts

depgraph.claim: authority lanes, dependency edges, committed paths, and public package roots remain valid; source-shape and inventory pressure is advisory
depgraph.failSafe: unclassified paths still run this gate and expand to full
depgraph.cost: small; 4.008 seconds in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
depgraph.escalation: move structural-pressure checks out if advisory noise obscures consequential dependency failures
depgraph.failureClass: Rust or TypeScript authority/import boundaries are violated or generated/public paths become unsafe
depgraph.representativeRegression: negative fixtures reject forbidden Rust/TypeScript edges and tracked build output

no-den-coupling.claim: engine remains independent of Den runtime code
no-den-coupling.failSafe: unclassified paths still run the gate
no-den-coupling.cost: tiny; 0.024 seconds in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
no-den-coupling.escalation: remove only if an explicit architecture decision makes Den a product dependency
no-den-coupling.failureClass: engine runtime or build becomes coupled to the task-control plane
no-den-coupling.representativeRegression: forbidden Den references fail the no-coupling scan

vocabulary.claim: ECRP vocabulary and Rust authority naming remain legible
vocabulary.trigger: every fast and full run while the scan remains cheap
vocabulary.failSafe: a true contract or authority violation is owned by contracts or depgraph instead
vocabulary.cost: small; 2.634 seconds in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
vocabulary.escalation: promote one precise rule only when a representative consequential API or authority ambiguity exists

identities.claim: shared execution fingerprints, receipts, and artifact identity
identities.trigger: harness identity/public-surface changes or scheduled/full closure
identities.failSafe: CI entrypoint changes expand to full
identities.cost: small; 1.661 seconds in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
identities.escalation: merge with CI policy if identity logic stops owning distinct collision/reuse behavior
identities.failureClass: stale execution reuse or duplicate artifact identity can hide the command actually tested
identities.representativeRegression: environment/input drift and cross-layer artifact collisions change or reject execution identity

bridge.claim: strict public wire and bridge boundary
bridge.trigger: bridge, protocol, or native boundary changes
bridge.failSafe: protocol changes select bridge validation; unknown changes expand to full
bridge.cost: medium; 10.121 seconds in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
bridge.escalation: split distribution checks only if they remain exact-revision and fail closed
bridge.failureClass: unknown or malformed public wire data reaches authority, or public glue drifts from the manifest
bridge.representativeRegression: missing, duplicate, mismatched, and unwired bridge fixtures fail

gameplay-conformance.claim: engine-owned gameplay provider behavior through public Rust seams
gameplay-conformance.trigger: scheduled/full closure and gameplay fixture changes
gameplay-conformance.failSafe: unknown fixture changes expand to full
gameplay-conformance.cost: small; 1.404 seconds in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
gameplay-conformance.escalation: move external-consumer scenarios to asha-testing; retain only concrete provider regressions
gameplay-conformance.failureClass: public gameplay bootstrap, invocation, state, or replay behavior breaks
gameplay-conformance.representativeRegression: negative composition fixtures and public provider save/replay checks fail

gameplay-sdk.claim: public gameplay SDK fixture and scaffold
gameplay-sdk.trigger: scheduled/full closure and public gameplay SDK changes
gameplay-sdk.failSafe: public-Rust changes select Rust and identity checks; unknown changes expand to full
gameplay-sdk.cost: small; 1.342 seconds in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
gameplay-sdk.escalation: merge with gameplay provider validation if scaffold generation ceases to be a distinct public claim
gameplay-sdk.failureClass: the published SDK cannot compose or scaffold a real provider
gameplay-sdk.representativeRegression: a freshly scaffolded module must compile and compose

replays.cost: tiny warm replay; 0.038 seconds in the 2026-07-16 full sample
replays.escalation: add a golden only for intentional durable product input, not report freshness
replays.failureClass: accepted state cannot be reproduced or atomic state transitions partially apply
replays.representativeRegression: committed replay checkpoints must reproduce exactly

render-goldens.cost: small; 0.521 seconds in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
render-goldens.escalation: remove a golden when it no longer represents a reviewed renderer-neutral product input
render-goldens.failureClass: renderer-neutral projection changes incompatibly or nondeterministically
render-goldens.representativeRegression: committed projection fixtures must reproduce exact snapshots

native.claim: native addon, strict wire, runtime bridge, and browser-host integration
native.trigger: native bridge changes, scheduled full CI, or campaign closure
native.failSafe: native paths select this gate; ambiguous CI paths expand to full
native.cost: large; 27.454 seconds including release rebuild in the 2026-07-16 warm full sample
native.escalation: narrow only if installed-addon and browser-host semantics remain distinct from reference tests
native.failureClass: the public native provider accepts invalid wire data or fails its installed runtime lifecycle
native.representativeRegression: strict N-API decode, installed-addon parity, browser-host lifecycle, and provider regressions fail closed

guardrail-policy.fallback: combine with full expansion for CI entrypoints

harness-policy.fallback: cross-cutting harness changes expand to full

harness-contract.fallback: unknown public fixtures expand to full

contract.fallback: generated-border changes select all border owners

source-shape-pressure.claim: large files and barrels signal review pressure
source-shape-pressure.trigger: affected language gate and scheduled/full closure
source-shape-pressure.failSafe: dependency, compile, and authority violations remain separate blockers
source-shape-pressure.escalation: name the hotspot and split candidate; never treat a line count as architecture truth

generated-inventory-freshness.claim: code maps and workspace counts remain useful navigation
generated-inventory-freshness.failSafe: actual dependency and public-root checks remain blocking
generated-inventory-freshness.escalation: repair during the owning change or schedule cleanup; retire inventory that becomes ambient churn

validator-negative-fixtures.claim: a changed validator still rejects its representative bad input
validator-negative-fixtures.trigger: harness-policy changes and scheduled/full closure; skipped on unrelated fast paths
validator-negative-fixtures.failSafe: unknown harness changes expand to full self-tests
validator-negative-fixtures.cost: medium only when harness self-tests are enabled
validator-negative-fixtures.escalation: merge duplicate fixtures that exercise the same rejection class
validator-negative-fixtures.failureClass: a consequential guard silently stops detecting its named invalid case
validator-negative-fixtures.representativeRegression: each retained authority, path, lint, and identity validator has a bounded negative fixture

computed-validation-reports.claim: per-run results describe one execution, not source truth
computed-validation-reports.failSafe: CI uploads ignored timing, receipt, and failure-tail artifacts

consumer-live-acceptance.claim: a person can use the visible gameplay or authoring result
consumer-live-acceptance.trigger: the owning user-facing task and repository
consumer-live-acceptance.failSafe: engine structural evidence cannot close the downstream task
consumer-live-acceptance.cost: measured in the owning consumer workflow
consumer-live-acceptance.escalation: route a concrete public-provider defect upstream with a focused regression

duplicate-execution-wrappers.claim: one normalized input/toolchain identity executes once
duplicate-execution-wrappers.failSafe: duplicate actual fingerprints invalidate the CI report
duplicate-execution-wrappers.cost: reduces rather than adds execution cost
duplicate-execution-wrappers.escalation: delete wrappers with no distinct semantic claim
duplicate-execution-wrappers.failureClass: duplicate execution wastes time or stale receipts misstate the tested surface
duplicate-execution-wrappers.representativeRegression: identity self-tests reject duplicate artifact IDs and environment-insensitive reuse
